hero.body: From the sap of the rubber tree to high-performance gloves that protect your hands. We bring Lalan Group's vertically integrated strength — from plantation to production — to Türkiye and the MENA region.

finder.title: Find the Right Glove
finder.body: Select your working environment and protection requirements — the best-matching models are listed instantly.

sustainability.body: Lalan is an FSC (Forest Stewardship Council) certified manufacturer. Lalan's sustainability framework ensures its rubber plantations protect biodiversity and support the communities that depend on them.

featured.body: A food-safe natural latex glove developed for food processing facilities. Its textured non-slip surface gives a secure grip, while the distinctive cobalt blue colour makes it easy to spot during food safety inspections — durability and comfort together.

global.title: Global Presence
global.body: From its headquarters in Sri Lanka, Lalan Group exports to more than 50 countries through offices across Europe, the Middle East, Asia and South America.

about.p1: Lalan Group is a multi-sector conglomerate built on vertical integration in the rubber industry. Its operations run from extensive rubber plantations through to world-class production facilities, placing it at the forefront of the sector.
about.p2: With a corporate presence across four continents, Lalan is recognised as a global authority in transforming raw latex into high-performance protective equipment and industrial solutions.

cards.plantationsDesc: Sustainable rubber from Lalan's own plantations, spanning over 17,000 acres.
cards.sustainabilityDesc: FSC-certified forestry, renewable energy and a zero deforestation policy.
cards.certificatesDesc: Independent quality credentials including ISO 9001, ISO 13485, FDA and FSC.

certs.body: Lalan Rubbers' commitment to international quality, safety and sustainability standards is certified by independent bodies.

history.intro: A trading venture that began in Sri Lanka in 1940 has become a global rubber giant operating across four continents and producing over 2 billion gloves a year.

milestones.title: A Journey That Began at a Trading Desk
milestones.desc: Peter Hapangama and his son David Hapangama launch rubber trading operations in the fertile soils of Sri Lanka. Sprouting from a small trading desk, this venture lays the foundation of the Lalan Group.

milestones.title: First International Export
milestones.desc: Lalan crosses the boundaries of domestic rubber trade and reaches international markets for the first time. The group's journey toward becoming a global player begins.

milestones.title: Third Generation, New Vision
milestones.desc: David Hapangama's son Lalith Hapangama joins the family business. Bringing value-added manufacturing concepts to the company, Lalith becomes the chief architect of Lalan's transformation into an industrial giant.

milestones.title: First Diversification: Printing & Packaging
milestones.desc: The first Lalan Printing and Packaging factory comes online. The company moves beyond its core rubber trade for the first time, laying the foundation of its multi-sector structure.

milestones.title: Lalan Rubbers Is Born
milestones.desc: As the second generation joins the family, the shift to value-added manufacturing begins. The first rubber glove production facility opens in Warakapola, Sri Lanka. Over time, glove manufacturing overtakes rubber trading to become the group's core business.

milestones.title: First Expansion: From 1 to 6 Facilities
milestones.desc: Lalan Rubbers' first production expansion period: from a single facility to six production sites. Capacity grows rapidly across Sri Lanka and the export network deepens.

milestones.title: Continued Growth
milestones.desc: The capacities of Lalan Rubbers and Lalan Printing & Packaging keep expanding. Product variety and export markets deepen; the group cements its place among Sri Lanka's leading industrial enterprises.

milestones.title: Strength From the Land: Plantations
milestones.desc: With the acquisition of a regional plantation company, Lalan steps into agriculture. With over 17,000 acres of rubber, tea, coconut and cinnamon plantations, vertical integration from raw material to finished product is achieved.

milestones.title: First International Expansion
milestones.desc: With the acquisition of various UK-based companies, the first commercial operations outside Sri Lanka are established. Diversification into engineering and tourism follows; the international corporate structure strengthens.

milestones.title: Second Expansion & Renewable Energy
milestones.desc: The second production expansion period: from six facilities to seven, with the portfolio widening from seamless work gloves to disposable nitrile gloves. The first investments in renewable energy are made. FSC® forest certification is obtained in 2010.

milestones.title: Third Expansion & Trade Across Four Continents
milestones.desc: The third production expansion program grows manufacturing capacity. The fourth generation of the family joins; international operations spread across four continents with the Lalan Malaysia, Lalan Brazil, Lalan Turkey and Lalan Middle East offices. Over 10,000 employees and an annual capacity exceeding 2 billion gloves.

aboutPage.heroBody: A multi-sector conglomerate built on vertical integration in the rubber industry. From plantation to product, from Sri Lanka to the world — over 80 years of expertise.
aboutPage.p1: Lalan Group is a multi-sector conglomerate built on vertical integration in the rubber industry. Its operations run from extensive rubber plantations through to world-class production facilities, placing it at the forefront of the sector.
aboutPage.p2: With a corporate presence across four continents, Lalan is recognised as a global authority in transforming raw latex into high-performance protective equipment and industrial solutions.
aboutPage.p3: Founded in Sri Lanka in 1940, the company today operates across agriculture, manufacturing, engineering and trade. Setting global standards in sustainable rubber sourcing and production, Lalan leads the sector with its FSC-certified plantations and ISO-approved facilities.
aboutPage.segmentsSub: From agriculture to trade, operations that span the entire value chain across four core business areas with a vertically integrated structure.

segments.title: Plantations & Agriculture
segments.desc: Rubber, tea, coconut and cinnamon plantations across more than 68 million m² of managed land. FSC-certified sustainable forestry practices.

segments.title: Rubber Manufacturing
segments.desc: Integrated production across 9 facilities, led by Sri Lanka, where natural latex is transformed into high-performance protective gloves and industrial products.

segments.title: Engineering Products
segments.desc: High-performance rubber engineering products for the automotive, construction and defence industries. Precise tolerances and full compliance with international standards.

segments.title: Trade & Distribution
segments.desc: A global sales and logistics network through authorised distributors in more than 50 countries. We serve as the authorised distributor for Türkiye and the MENA region.

timeline.title: Founding
timeline.desc: Peter Hapangama and his son David launch rubber trading operations in the fertile soils of Sri Lanka.

timeline.title: First International Export
timeline.desc: Expansion from domestic trade to international markets — the first step of global growth.

timeline.title: Lalan Rubbers Is Born
timeline.desc: The first rubber glove production facility opens in Warakapola; glove manufacturing becomes the group's core business.

timeline.title: Rapid Expansion
timeline.desc: From 1 facility to 6; the export network deepens and capacity grows rapidly across Sri Lanka.

timeline.title: Global Offices
timeline.desc: International offices open in London, Istanbul, Oman, Malaysia and Brazil; direct exports to more than 50 countries begin.

timeline.title: World Leader
timeline.desc: An annual capacity exceeding 2 billion gloves, 10,000+ employees and sustainable rubber solutions across 4 continents.

sustainabilityPage.intro: Sustainability is not a principle Lalan Group adopted later; it is a value carried since the founding of the business. Lalan protects forests, supports local communities and leaves behind a responsible raw-material chain for the future.

pillars.title: FSC® Certified Forest Management
pillars.desc: The FSC® (Forest Stewardship Council) certification has been renewed without interruption since 2010. Lalan's plantations are managed to protect biodiversity, water resources and local communities.

pillars.title: Zero Deforestation Policy
pillars.desc: All of Lalan's plantation areas are planned to preserve existing forest land. Not destroying natural habitats is among Lalan's core principles.

pillars.title: Vertical Integration & Traceability
pillars.desc: Latex produced from Lalan's own plantations provides full traceability from raw material to finished product. Every step of the supply chain is under independent audit.

pillars.title: 100% Biodegradable Latex
pillars.desc: Natural rubber latex, unlike petroleum-based synthetic alternatives, is fully biodegradable. The majority of Lalan products are made from this sustainable raw material.

pillars.title: Community Support
pillars.desc: Through the Lalith Hapangama Foundation, education, health and infrastructure support is provided to local communities in plantation regions. Small farmers are given saplings and a buy-back guarantee.

pillars.title: Renewable Energy
pillars.desc: Through investments in the renewable energy sector during 2009–2014, the environmental impact of energy consumption at production facilities is being reduced.

sustainabilityPage.fscP1: The FSC® (Forest Stewardship Council) is the world's most trusted forest-management certification body. Since 2010, Lalan has maintained this certification without interruption through an independent audit body accredited by the Germany-based FSC.
sustainabilityPage.fscP2: Lalan's FSC certification ensures that its plantations protect biodiversity, conserve water resources and support the livelihoods of local communities.
sustainabilityPage.fscP3: FSC® certified products are available on request. Please contact us for certificate details and product scope.

plantationsPage.intro: With over 17,500 acres of plantation land since 2003, Lalan is one of the rare manufacturers that controls its raw material. Lalan's latex grows on its own soil, to its own standards.
plantationsPage.cropsSub: While the rubber tree (Hevea brasiliensis) is Lalan's core crop, several tropical crops are cultivated as part of its diversification strategy.

crops.name: Rubber
crops.desc: The core crop. Integrated rubber plantations that ensure traceability of natural latex throughout the entire production process.

crops.name: Tea
crops.desc: A significant part of Sri Lanka's world-renowned Ceylon tea production.

crops.name: Coconut
crops.desc: Coconut plantations grown in the tropical climate are part of the diversified agricultural portfolio.

crops.name: Cinnamon
crops.desc: Sri Lanka cinnamon (Cinnamomum verum), a high-quality product for the gourmet and pharmaceutical industries.

crops.name: Forestry
crops.desc: FSC-certified sustainable forestry practices; protecting biodiversity is a core principle.

plantationsPage.qualitySub: At Lalan, quality is the responsibility of the entire organisation, not a single department.

qualityItems.title: Raw-Material Traceability
qualityItems.desc: Latex obtained from Lalan's own plantations can be tracked at every stage from source to finished product. This integrated structure makes it possible to keep raw-material quality under direct control and to comply with international standards.

qualityItems.title: Central Rubber Nursery (CRN)
qualityItems.desc: The CRN, which holds the distinction of being Sri Lanka's largest rubber nursery, is certified by the Rubber Development Department of Sri Lanka. High-yield saplings and a buy-back guarantee are offered to small farmers.

qualityItems.title: Independent Audit & Certification
qualityItems.desc: All quality standards (ISO, FSC, BRC, etc.) are verified through annual audits by independent accreditation bodies. Transparency and accountability are among Lalan's corporate values.

qualityItems.title: Continuous Improvement
qualityItems.desc: Lalan's R&D activities, product development processes and production technologies are continuously updated. Growth from 1 facility to 7 modern facilities since 1988 is a concrete demonstration of this mindset.

plantationsPage.docsSub: Lalan's FSC® certification documents, plantation management plans and agri division policies.

docs.title: Agri Division Policies 2022
docs.desc: Lalan Rubbers Agri Division's current policy document — environment, occupational safety, chemical use and FSC compliance standards.

docs.title: Agri Division Policies 2021
docs.desc: Amendment IV — operational policies applied to rubber, forest and agricultural areas under FSC certification.

docs.title: FSC® Forest Management Plan 2018–22
docs.desc: FSC Manual: the five-year forest management plan and sustainability targets for Lalan's 68.8 million m² certified area.

plantationsPage.crnBody: The Central Rubber Nursery (CRN) is certified by the Rubber Development Department of Sri Lanka and holds the title of the country's largest nursery. The CRN supplies high-yield rubber saplings to small farmers and, through a buy-back guarantee, helps local growers earn a sustainable income.

certsPage.intro: Lalan Rubbers has its compliance with international quality, safety, social responsibility and environmental standards regularly audited by independent bodies.
certsPage.footerBody: Official certificate copies and compliance documents are provided on request.

ISO 9001.desc: The international quality management system standard that ensures Lalan's products and services consistently meet customer and regulatory requirements.

ISO 13485.desc: A certificate documenting that Lalan's quality management system for the laboratory and medical device sector complies with international standards. Mandatory for medical-grade gloves.

FDA.desc: Lalan's food-safe gloves are certified under FDA Title 21 CFR, documenting that the glove components comply with food safety regulations and contain "substances generally recognized as safe for use in food or food packaging."

FDA 510(k).desc: Demonstrates that Lalan gloves marketed as medical devices in the US have successfully completed the FDA premarket notification process (510k) and meet safety and efficacy requirements.

Health Canada.desc: Approval from Canada's federal health authority documenting that Lalan gloves meet the requirements under the Canadian Food and Drugs Act (FDA) and its Regulations (FDR).

REACH.desc: Documents that Lalan adopts the EU chemicals regulation and manages the risks to human health and the environment of the chemicals used in production in line with EU regulations.

BSCI.desc: Using the BSCI social audit methodology, Lalan continuously improves and maintains working conditions across all its facilities at the highest level. Covers human rights, ethical business practices and supply chain transparency.

Sedex.desc: Sedex (Supplier Ethical Data Exchange) allows Lalan to catalogue and share information on its ethical and social compliance performance with its partners, ensuring a sustainable and transparent supply chain.

BRC Global Standards.desc: A global standard for food-sector suppliers. Under BRC Consumer Products Certification, Lalan documents the safety, legality and quality of product design as well as the food-safety suitability of the production environment.

GOTS.desc: GOTS is an organic textile production certification that restricts the use of toxic bleaches, dyes and other chemicals in Lalan's production process. It covers the entire chain from raw material to labelling.

ASTM International.desc: Lalan applies ASTM standards to improve product quality, strengthen health and safety, facilitate market access and demonstrate to customers that they can rely on the products.

Sanitized®.desc: The SANITIZED® antimicrobial treatment is applied to selected Lalan products to protect gloves against the colonisation and proliferation of bacteria, mould, mildew, algae or dust mites.
